On this page

Social Implications — Quick Summary ​

No prose. Tables and key facts for all social implications topics.

Copyright & Intellectual Property ​

TypeProtectsDuration
CopyrightCreative works (music, code, writing, art)Life + 50–70 years
PatentInventions, processes20 years
TrademarkLogos, brand namesRenewable indefinitely

Copyright gives the owner exclusive right to: copy, distribute, modify, sell, or licence the work.

Software Licences ​

TypeFree?Source?Copy allowed?
ProprietaryPaidClosedNo
FreewareYesClosedUsually yes
SharewareTrialClosedTrial only
Open sourceUsuallyOpenYes (conditions)
Public domainYesOpenYes, no restrictions

Creative Commons Conditions ​

CodeMeaning
BYMust credit original creator
SADerivative must use same licence
NCNo commercial use
NDNo modifications allowed

Piracy Types ​

  • End-user piracy (installing on more machines than licensed)
  • Counterfeiting (fake copies)
  • Internet piracy (illegal downloads/torrents)
  • Hard disk loading (pre-installing on PCs for sale)

Consequences: fines, criminal charges, civil lawsuit, malware risk

Cybercrime Types ​

CrimeDescription
HackingUnauthorised access to computer/network
VirusAttaches to files; spreads when file shared
WormSelf-replicates across networks; no user action needed
TrojanDisguised as legitimate software; opens backdoor
RansomwareEncrypts files; demands payment
SpywareSecretly monitors user; logs keystrokes
PhishingFake emails/sites to steal credentials
Identity theftUsing another person's personal info for fraud
DDoSFlooding server with traffic to crash it
SQL injectionInserting malicious SQL into web forms
SpoofingFaking sender identity
CyberbullyingHarassing/threatening via technology

Virus vs Worm vs Trojan ​

VirusWormTrojan
Spreads byInfected file sharingNetwork, no user actionDeceptive download
Disguise?NoNoYes (looks legitimate)
Replicates?Yes (needs host file)Yes (self-contained)No

Protection Measures ​

Technical:

  • Antivirus / anti-malware
  • Firewall (hardware or software)
  • Regular software updates/patches
  • Encryption + HTTPS
  • Two-factor authentication (2FA)
  • Strong, unique passwords
  • VPN
  • Regular backups

Human:

  • Don't click suspicious links
  • Verify sender email addresses
  • Never give OTPs/passwords by phone
  • Check URL before entering credentials (padlock, https)
  • Be cautious about what you share on social media

Phishing Signs ​

  • Urgent language ("Act now or your account will be closed")
  • Sender email doesn't match the claimed organisation
  • Hover over link — URL doesn't match what's shown
  • Grammar/spelling errors
  • Requests for password, OTP, or card details

South African Legislation ​

ActCovers
Copyright Act (1978)Creative work protection
ECT Act (2002)Cybercrime, electronic contracts
Cybercrimes Act (2020)Hacking, ransomware, unlawful access
POPIA (2013)Protection of personal information

Privacy & Ethics ​

TermDefinition
PrivacyRight to control who sees your personal information
Digital footprintTrail of data left by online activity
CookieSmall file stored in browser tracking visits/preferences
POPIARequires organisations to protect personal data; fines up to R10M

Digital Divide ​

The gap between those with and without access to ICT:

Causes: cost of devices/connectivity, lack of infrastructure, digital literacy, geography (rural vs urban)

Effects: inequality in education, employment, healthcare, government services

Environmental Impact of ICT ​

IssueDetail
E-wasteDiscarded electronics contain toxic materials (lead, mercury)
Energy consumptionData centres use enormous electricity
Carbon footprintManufacturing and running devices produces emissions
SolutionsGreen data centres (renewable energy), e-waste recycling, buying refurbished

ICT in Society ​

TechnologySocial impact
AI / automationJob displacement; new job creation; medical diagnosis
RoboticsManufacturing automation; reduces dangerous human work
IoTSmart homes/cities; privacy concerns
Social mediaCommunication; cyberbullying; misinformation
Remote workFlexibility; work-life balance challenges
